question_title: Will Virgin Galactic have a "Going Concern" substantial doubt statement in its 10-Q SEC filing for Q2 2026?
summary: Virgin Galactic's Q1 2026 10-Q already carried an explicit going concern statement. The company remains pre-revenue (commercial service not until Q4 2026), burns ~$90M/quarter, and had only ~2 quarters of runway (~$220M unrestricted at Mar 31 minus ~$90M Q2 burn plus ~$52M ATM). ASC 205-40 requires demonstrating sufficient liquidity for 12 months, and management explicitly excludes capital-market transactions (not within its control) from its evaluation — structurally guaranteeing the doubt persists absent a large committed financing. The Q2 debt exchanges reduced leverage but were mostly dilutive equity swaps, not cash injections. Only a decisive committed strategic investment (~$300M+) would flip this to NO, which is speculative. Tail risks (filing timing, annulment, surprise financing) keep me at 95% rather than ~99%.

factors.description: Q1 2026 10-Q already contained an explicit going concern (ASC 205-40) statement — status quo strongly favors continuation
Company remains pre-revenue (commercial service not until Q4 2026), burning ~$90M/quarter with only ~2 quarters of runway — cannot demonstrate 12-month liquidity, the core ASC 205-40 test
Q2 2026 capital activity was mostly dilutive debt-for-stock exchanges plus modest ATM raises — reduced debt but did not add 12+ months of cash to remove the doubt
Small chance of a large committed strategic investment (rumored new investor) that provides 12-month runway and lets management remove the qualification
Small risk the Q2 10-Q is not filed before resolution or the question is annulled

reasoning:
**Forecast: 95% (Yes)**

Virgin Galactic will almost certainly retain its going concern statement in the Q2 2026 10-Q. The company already disclosed one in Q1 2026, remains pre-revenue until Q4 2026, burns ~$90M/quarter, and has only ~2 quarters of runway — far short of the 12-month liquidity ASC 205-40 requires. Its Q2 balance-sheet actions were mostly dilutive debt-for-equity swaps that cut leverage but didn't extend cash runway. Only a large committed strategic financing would remove the doubt, which is speculative. I held at 95% rather than higher to account for tail risks (filing timing, a surprise financing, or annulment).
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if, in its 10-Q SEC [filing](https://www.sec.gov/edgar/browse/?CIK=1706946) for the quarterly period ended June 30, 2026, Virgin Galactic Holdings, Inc. has a statement expressing substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern, pursuant to [ASC 205-40](https://viewpoint.pwc.com/dt/us/en/pwc/accounting_guides/financial_statement_/financial_statement___18_US/chapter_24_risks_and_US/245_going_concern_US.html).
fine_print: This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/44247) which opened on 2026-06-26 23:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.

factors.description: Q1 2026 10-Q already contained an explicit going concern (ASC 205-40) statement — status quo strongly favors continuation
Company remains pre-revenue (commercial service not until Q4 2026), burning ~$90M/quarter with only ~2 quarters of runway — cannot demonstrate 12-month liquidity, the core ASC 205-40 test
Q2 2026 capital activity was mostly dilutive debt-for-stock exchanges plus modest ATM raises — reduced debt but did not add 12+ months of cash to remove the doubt
Small chance of a large committed strategic investment (rumored new investor) that provides 12-month runway and lets management remove the qualification
Small risk the Q2 10-Q is not filed before resolution or question annulled

input.anchor: Base rate for going concern statement persisting into the very next quarterly filing for a pre-revenue cash-burning company: ~90-95%. Anchor logit ~2.5.
input.assessment: Going concern statements for pre-revenue cash-burning companies almost never disappear in the immediately following quarter. Virgin Galactic had it in Q1 2026, burns ~$90M/quarter, has only ~2 quarters of runway, and won't have revenue until Q4 2026. ASC 205-40 requires demonstrating sufficient liquidity for 12 months; management explicitly excludes capital market transactions (not within its control) from its evaluation, which structurally guarantees the doubt persists unless a large committed financing lands. The Q2 debt exchanges were dilutive equity swaps, not cash injections. Only a very large committed strategic investment would flip this to NO — speculative and unlikely by the Q2 filing.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether a large committed strategic financing (rumored) closes and provides 12-month runway before the Q2 filing; whether the 10-Q is filed before the resolution window.
input.tool_audit: research() returned useful financial data (cash, burn, debt exchanges) but erroneously claimed no going concern in Q1 2026 — contradicted by the question text which quotes the Q1 10-Q going concern statement directly. I trusted the primary quote over the sub-agent summary.
input.process_reflection: The research sub-agent conflated press-release summaries with the actual 10-Q, producing a factual error on the central variable (going concern presence). Fortunately the question text contained the authoritative quote. For definitional/accounting-continuity questions like this, the status quo from the prior filing plus cash-runway math is sufficient; no heavy simulation needed.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified all five factors against the research trace and worldview store. No errors found that would change the forecast.

FACTOR GROUNDING:
- Factor 1 (Q1 2026 10-Q had going concern): The research sub-agent actually claimed the OPPOSITE ("No explicit going-concern qualification in the Q1 2026 materials"). The agent correctly overrode this using the authoritative question text, which quotes the Q1 going concern statement directly, and disclosed the discrepancy transparently in both its response and tool_audit. This is the right call, not a hallucination — the status-quo claim is grounded in the resolution criteria/background.
- Factor 2 (pre-revenue, ~$90M/qtr burn, ~2 quarters runway, revenue Q4 2026): Fully grounded — Q1 FCF -$93M, Q2 guidance -$87M/-$92M, ~$220M unrestricted liquidity, first commercial flights Q4 2026.
- Factor 3 (Q2 capital activity mostly dilutive debt-for-stock + modest ATM): Grounded — June 10 first-lien redemption in stock, June 22 convertible exchange for stock+warrants, 9.80% note swap for shares, ~$52M April ATM. Correctly characterized as deleveraging, not cash runway extension.
- Factor 4 (small chance of large strategic investment): Grounded — phemex source notes "reports of a new strategic investor" and the 125% June stock spike. Correctly signed negative.
- Factor 5 (filing/annulment tail): Reasonable, correctly signed.

RESOLUTION ALIGNMENT: Criteria are provided (ASC 205-40, Q2 2026 10-Q). Factors engage directly with the specific 12-month-liquidity test and the exact filing. Good.

PRE-PUBLICATION EVENT: The Q2 capital actions are June 2026 (pre-publish July 3), but the agent uses them only as forward-looking evidence about the not-yet-filed Q2 10-Q — it does NOT treat any event as already resolving the question. No Case 2 violation.

WORLDVIEW/CONSISTENCY: The only SPCE worldview entry is the same research trace used here. The other two distress-company research entries cover Beyond Meat and a basket (Faraday Future, JetBlue, etc.), not Virgin Galactic — no contradictions.

COUNTERARGUMENT: Genuine — constructs the coherent NO story (balance-sheet flurry, rumored investor, June rally, precedent of removing language before milestones). Not a token gesture.

SELF-ASSESSMENT: Confidence 0.85 is calibrated given the strong status-quo anchor tempered by real tail risks. Tool_audit honestly flags the sub-agent error; metrics (0 errors) are consistent.

PROBABILITY: My independent estimate aligns with ~95%. Going concern language virtually never disappears in the immediately following quarter for a pre-revenue company burning ~$90M/quarter with ~2 quarters of runway and no revenue until Q4 2026; ASC 205-40 excludes not-within-control capital-market transactions, and the Q2 exchanges were dilutive swaps, not runway-extending cash. 95% sits appropriately below the ~99% factor sum, accounting for filing-timing and strategic-investment tail risk. The departure from the ~90-95% anchor is modest and evidence-driven.
